header.title: Voices of Innovation
header.subtitle: Browse the community builders, technologists, and storytellers sharing their experience on stage at WTM Montreal 2026.

list.name: Afaf Taïk
list.bio: Dr. Afaf Taïk is an assistant professor at the Université de Sherbrooke and currently a postdoctoral researcher at Mila – Quebec AI Institute and the Université de Montréal, working on the intersection of fairness and privacy in machine learning.

list.name: Alexandra Nemery
list.bio:
Alexandra Nemery is a professor and researcher in user experience (UX) design at the École de technologie supérieure (ÉTS) in Montreal. With a PhD in ergonomics, she specializes in persuasive design, gamification, and user experience evaluation in complex contexts such as serious games, virtual reality, and interactive scientific tools. 
 With extensive professional experience in the video game industry (Ubisoft, Square Enix, Haven Studio), she now conducts research projects at the interface of science, technology, and design, particularly focusing on content gamification, explainable AI, and ethical and inclusive design.

list.name: Angie Ariza
list.bio: Angie Ariza is the AI Lead Developer at Plus Company, where she leads the Smart Persona project and specializes in scaling AI systems from proof-of-concept to production. Her expertise spans Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), AI agents, distributed systems, and generative models, with a strong track record of turning cutting-edge research into production-ready solutions. She has led impactful projects across fraud detection, forecasting, and large-scale data platforms. Beyond her technical leadership, Angie is a Women Techmakers Ambassador, committed to advancing the visibility and inclusion of women in technology.

list.name: Annalise Iten
list.bio: Annalise is an Employment Specialist at YES Montreal, helping people navigate their career paths and overcome barriers to employment in the Montreal area.

list.name: Anne Nguyen
list.bio: Principal Director responsible for artificial intelligence at the Quebec Innovation Council, Anne Nguyen works to make AI a lever for responsible innovation, aligned with the values of inclusion, equality and humanity that guide her career.

list.name: Applied AI Institute
list.bio: The Applied AI Institute at Concordia University is dedicated to advancing the field of Artificial Intelligence through interdisciplinary research, education, and community engagement, with a focus on ethical and inclusive AI development.

list.name: Ayrat Khayretdivov
list.bio: Ayrat Khayretdinov is a Software Engineer at Google and an active member of the AI Tinkerers Montreal community.

list.name: Chloé Freslon
list.bio: Chloé Freslon is the founder of URelles, a company offering innovative solutions to support organizations aspiring to a more inclusive and equitable culture.

list.name: Claude Cardinal
list.bio:
A doctoral candidate in computer science (video game project management) at UQAC, Claude Cardinal has a diverse background in computer science, education, and public administration. He contributed to skills development at Eidos Montreal. 
 With over thirty years of experience in education and instructional design, he previously directed the CeFTI at the University of Sherbrooke and worked as a programmer-analyst, application architect, and project manager in various technological environments.

list.name: Dr. Sabrine Amri
list.bio: Dr. Sabrine Amri is a postdoctoral researcher at Polytechnique Montréal, specializing in cybersecurity and AI safety.

list.name: Dre. Nadia Seraiocco
list.bio: Nadia Seraiocco, PhD in Communication, jointly holds degrees from Concordia University, the University of Montreal, and UQAM. After a career in communications from 1999 to 2014, working as a journalist and for organizations such as the National Gallery of Canada, VIA Rail, and the City of Montreal, her curiosity about technology led her to develop expertise in digital communications.

list.name: François Legaré
list.bio: François Legare is a Lead Developer at Bell and a contributor to the AI Tinkerers Montreal community.

list.name: Ghislaine Boundjia
list.bio: Ghislaine Boundjia is an IT Project Management Consultant and the founder of Black Ladies in Tech (BLiT).

list.name: JL Maréchaux
list.bio:
JL Marechaux is a Principal AI Consultant at ServiceNow. He brings extensive experience in applied artificial intelligence and digital transformation.​  
 During his career, JL has held a diverse range of roles, including AI technical advisor, Cloud specialist, head of technology and principal enterprise architect in tech companies like Google, Ivado Labs, Blue Yonder and IBM. He was also a course lecturer at McGill University.​  
 In his last role prior to joining ServiceNow, JL was part of the 'Solutions & Thought Leadership' team at Google, helping North American companies use artificial intelligence to accelerate their digital transformation. He worked on advanced analytics and applied machine learning (ML) solutions for clients across key sectors such as retail, finance, travel, automotive, and insurance.​  
 JL actively contributes to the Montreal technology community as a mentor, a public speaker, an ambassador for Women in Data Science (WiDS) and co-organizer of the AI Tinkerers Montréal group.

list.name: Khaoula Chehbouni
list.bio: Khaoula Chehbouni is a third-year PhD student at McGill University and Mila (Quebec AI Institute). She received the prestigious FRQNT doctoral training scholarship to conduct research on fairness and security in large language models.

list.name: Mélisane Lafrenaye
list.bio: Mélisane Lafrenaye is a developer and technical team lead at the video game studio Triple Boris.

list.name: Mahaua Chowdhury
list.bio: Mahaua Chowdhury is a Quebec-born engineering professional with nearly ten years of experience in engineering consulting, technical training, and digital transformation.

list.name: Mahsa Rezaei
list.bio:
Mahsa is a Future of Work Architect and Social Entrepreneur with over 15 years of experience, evolving from Tech to leader in building career resilience for women in the AI economy. 
 As founder of 'MamaNest', she has pioneered two flagship events 'MamaTech AI Studio', and the 'Women's Career Conference' alongside other activities to build a comprehensive career-growth ecosystem for women.

list.name: Mahzad Kalantari
list.bio: Mahzad Kalantari is a machine learning and data science expert with a PhD in computer science and computer vision and over 20 years of experience in applied machine learning and data science. She works in the video game industry, where her focus is on integrating reinforcement learning into games to build intelligent systems, support game development, and enhance player experiences.

list.name: Marie-Gabrielle Ménard
list.bio: Marie-Gabrielle Ménard, Liberal MP for Hochelaga—Rosemont-East, Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for Women and Gender Equality and Secretary of State (Small Business and Tourism). Before making the leap into politics, Ms. Ménard made a name for herself as an artist, reporter and host, notably on radio and television. An experienced communicator, woman of heart and action, she is committed with conviction to carrying the voice of the population. We will have the privilege of hearing her perspective on the place of women in work environments that are still predominantly male.

list.name: Maya Toussaint
list.bio: Maya Toussaint is a DEI Consultant and speaker, specializing in inclusive hiring practices and building equitable workplace cultures.

list.name: Melanie Guerin
list.bio: Mélanie Guérin is a principal partner at FJNR, where she accompanies organizations and entrepreneurs in the design and realization of digital projects.

list.name: Michell Payano
list.bio: Michell Payano started her career as an Economist in the public sector in Dominican Republic, where she developed a foundation in data driven policymaking. Recognizing the transformative potential of AI to address complex societal challenges, she made a transition to technology by pursuing a Master's degree in Computer Science with a specialization in machine learning at Université de Montréal, in collaboration with Mila.

list.name: Nadia Tahiri
list.bio: Nadia Tahiri received her M.Sc. and Ph.D. degrees in Computer Science from the University of Quebec at Montreal. She is currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science at the University of Sherbrooke.

list.name: Nathalie Sanon
list.bio: Nathalie Sanon is a manager specializing in AI training and its social impacts.

list.name: Nicole Kenmei
list.bio: Nicole Kenmei is a cybersecurity consultant and co-founder of Black Ladies in Tech (BLiT).

list.name: Noémi Mercier
list.bio: Noémi Mercier is a journalist and host. With a particular interest in social and political issues, she has led several documentaries and series as a host, content producer or director, including 'IA: l'angle mort'.

list.name: Nogol Madani
list.bio: Nogol Madani is a future-focused workplace expert, speaker, and facilitator. She is the founder and CEO of a company dedicated to helping leaders build high-performing, inclusive, future-ready teams through psychological safety.

list.name: Pierre-Alexandre Lacerte
list.bio: Pierre-Alexandre Lacerte is a Software Engineer at Upgrade and an active contributor to the AI Tinkerers Montreal community.

list.name: Pierre-Luc Labbée
list.bio: Pierre-Luc Labbée is the founder of rhum. An ICF-certified coach with 20 years in Tech, Gaming, and VFX, Pierre-Luc’s superpower is cutting through the noise with common sense to build human-first strategies.

list.name: Raphaëlle Giraud
list.bio: Raphaëlle Giraud is a Team Lead in Data Engineering at Cirque du Soleil Entertainment Group, where she leads data and AI initiatives.

list.name: Rashida Geddes
list.bio: Rashida is a talent acquisition leader at Bell, focused on identifying and nurturing diverse talent in the telecommunications and tech sectors.

list.name: Reem Habib
list.bio: Reem Habib is a student at the Université de Montréal and an active member of the Women Techmakers Montreal organizing team. She serves as the moderator for the community panel on Tech Trajectories.

list.name: Sabine Pierre
list.bio: Sabine Pierre is a Lead Backend Engineer and AI Developer at Bell. She is an expert in building scalable backend systems.

list.name: Stéphanie Akré
list.bio: Stéphanie Akré is a social entrepreneur and co-founder of Ludociels pour tous, a social economy organization that helps design inclusive digital tools. Her professional journey has been marked by a significant transition: after experiencing burnout, she chose to redirect her career towards social entrepreneurship and learn on the job the skills related to managing an organization, digital communication, and collective entrepreneurship.

list.name: Stéphanie Jecrois
list.bio: Stéphanie Jecrois is the co-founder of Technovation Montréal, a non-profit organization that aims to develop technology and entrepreneurship skills in girls aged 8 to 18.

list.name: Stacy Véronneau
list.bio: I wear many hats, but my primary role is to architect cloud solutions with a focus on security and infrastructure. In this capacity, I've honed my competencies by defining technical pre-sales processes and engaging with customers to tailor cloud offerings that address their unique challenges and drive growth.

list.name: Sta Kuzviwanza
list.bio: Sta Kuzviwanza represents PERT Quebec and participates in the discussion on breaking down tech talent barriers for English-speaking women.

list.name: Stefania Pecore
list.bio: Stefania Pecore is the Executive Director of Women Techmakers Montreal. With over a decade of experience leading the Montreal community, she is dedicated to building inclusive spaces for women in technology.

list.name: Thomas Gaudy
list.bio: Thomas Gaudy is a UX designer, computer science researcher, and co-founder of the social economy organization Ludociels pour tous (Games for All), which works to promote the inclusion and accessibility of digital interfaces. He holds a PhD in computer science and specializes in the study of digital ergonomics, cognitive and social psychology, and experimental games, leading him to design accessible video games, particularly for audiences with various disabilities.

list.name: Ulrich Aïvodji
list.bio: Ulrich is an assistant professor in computer science at ÉTS in the department of software engineering and information technology. His research areas are Responsible AI, privacy protection and cybersecurity.

list.name: Vivienne Suen
list.bio: Vivienne Suen is a cybersecurity architect with a flair for storytelling and over 15 years' experience advising IT organizations on solutions and strategy.
